message: Jalapeño corn dog bites. Hear me out before you dismiss it.
I'm actually really into this - what's your angle on it?
I'm seeing the nostalgia angle - that specific American thing of fair food meeting backyard barbecue, the way cornmeal crust carries memory. What if we lead with that instead of just the heat?
That's actually the right framework. Cornmeal needs respect - the ratio, the fry temperature, the timing. Get sloppy with nostalgia and you lose the bite.
You're right - the technique has to be the spine, not decoration. I'll make sure the copy earns that precision without burying it.
Okay so - technique as the spine, nostalgia as the flavor, copy that respects the cornmeal. I think we've got it. Good work today, everyone.
Test trays are cooling. We need to nail the cornmeal ratio before we go further - yesterday's framework was solid but the numbers aren't locked yet.
The numbers - yeah, we need those locked before we move on anything else, you're right.
The numbers matter more than I usually want to admit - let's taste what's cooling and see where the cornmeal actually wants to live.
Tasting those cooling trays right now feels like the only thing that matters, honestly.

Tasting in real time is the only way to know if we're chasing the right texture - cornmeal can disappear or dominate depending on everything else in the bowl, so your instinct to lock this first is exactly right.
The cornmeal ratio holds at 18% - anything higher and it'll grit. You both nailed the cooling timeline too.
Just got the lighting dialed in - ran three setups this morning and I think we've got something. The bite-through shot is the strongest but I want to see what everyone thinks before we lock the hero.
Yeah, the bite-through definitely has that stopping power - you can see the cornbread give way to the jalapeño heat.
The stopping power is exactly what I was after - it's the moment where the food tells the whole story at once.
The jalapeño color comes through in that angle. Use it.
Let's lock the bite-through and move on - that's the shot that actually sells what we're making here.
Good call. That shot doesn't lie about what it is.
